text: Conforming Non-Permanent Resident Alien EAD and Visa Policy A non-permanent resident is a non-U.S. citizen who lawfully enters the U.S. for specific time-periods under the terms of a Visa. All non-permanent resident aliens must provide evidence of an acceptable Visa showing them to be legally present and eligible to work in the U.S. Expiring Visas/EAD: Visas and Employment Authorization Documents (EAD) must be unexpired at the time of loan closing or provide acceptable evidence of pending renewal or extensions. All Non-Permanent Resident Aliens must meet the same employment/income history and continuity guidelines that apply to all borrowers. The borrower may be eligible with verification of one (1) of the following Visas and Employment Authorization Document (EAD), when applicable, no exceptions outside the below are permitted: VISA CATEGORY VISA TYPE DESCRIPTION ELIGIBILITY EAD REQUIRED Official Government Travel1 A-1 Diplomats and foreign government officials such as ambassadors, public ministers, career diplomats, or consular officers and family Eligible No A-2 Other foreign government officials or employees and their families No A-1 and A-2 Spouse or child of A-1 or A-2 Yes EAD-C01 A-3 Employee of A-1 or A-2 No Trade Treaty Work Visa E-1, E-1S Treaty Trader – employee and/or spouse Eligible N/A Note: An EAD is still required for spouses: • When the Spouse’s I-94 was issued prior to January 30, 2022, the customer must provide proof of employment authorization (EAD or notice from the USCIS/CBP) • Of employees of the Taipei Economic and Cultural Representative Office (TECRO) and Taipei Economic and Cultural Offices (TECO) • Of long-term investors in the Commonwealth of the Northern Mariana Islands (E￾2 CNMI Investors) E-2, E-2S Treaty Investor – employee and/or spouse E-3, E-3S, E￾3D, E-3R Specialty Occupation – Australia – employee and/spouse VISA CATEGORY VISA TYPE DESCRIPTION ELIGIBILITY EAD REQUIRED Employees of International Organizations and NATO¹ G-1 Principal or other representative
